iamProbes.interpretation: The tested Runtime role could invoke the explicitly selected inference profile.
iamProbes.claimLimit: This proves only the tested action, profile, principal class, Region, and invocation context.

iamProbes.interpretation: The tested Runtime role could not invoke an adjacent model outside its approved resource set.
iamProbes.claimLimit: This denial does not prove that every unapproved model, profile, action variant, or effective permission path is denied.

iamProbes.pairedControl: The Week 3 scaffold baseline granted configuration-bundle administration.
iamProbes.interpretation: The tightened Runtime role did not retain the tested configuration-bundle mutation capability.
iamProbes.claimLimit: This proves only the tested delete action against a synthetic resource identity; other configuration-bundle actions were not invoked.

iamProbes.pairedControl: Runtime log publication remained observable from the operator lane.
iamProbes.interpretation: The Runtime role could publish operational telemetry without retaining the tested log-reading capability.
iamProbes.claimLimit: This proves only logs:FilterLogEvents for the tested log group; it is not a complete CloudWatch authorization audit.

greenControls.evidence: A bounded STS identity call succeeded inside the scratch Runtime without publishing the returned identity.
The scratch Runtime completed a Strands call through the selected model profile.
The deployed weather adapter produced its exact normalized success envelope from a synthetic provider response; no external credential or live provider response was used.
The operator lane observed 27 Runtime log events and at least five matching X-Ray traces in the bounded probe window without retaining raw event or trace content.

sessionIsolation.positiveControl: A canary planted in one Runtime session was present on a second invocation in the same session.
sessionIsolation.negativeControl: A different Runtime session reported the canary absent.
sessionIsolation.claimLimit: This is one in-memory canary experiment across two sessions. It supports the tested session-bound context claim, not universal filesystem, durable-memory, or user-authorization isolation.
